senses.definition: a vehicle designed to transport people rather than goods

examples.x: The passenger vehicle had seating for up to five adults.
He rented a comfortable passenger vehicle for his family's road trip.
They parked their passenger vehicle in the designated area at the airport.
She preferred traveling by passenger vehicle rather than taking public transit.
The city's regulations required all passenger vehicles to undergo regular inspections.

senses.definition: a large vehicle that carries many passengers by road

examples.x: I prefer sitting near the window when I'm on the bus.
The bus arrived just in time, so I didn't have to wait long.
The bus took us to the city center where we explored the shops and attractions.
The bus was full, so I had to stand for the entire journey.

senses.definition: a fast and efficient public transportation system with dedicated lanes for buses, modern stations, and streamlined fare collection

examples.x: Many cities around the world are investing in bus rapid transit to improve urban transportation.

senses.definition: a public transportation vehicle powered by electricity, typically with batteries or overhead lines, emitting zero tailpipe emissions

examples.x: The city council decided to replace its diesel fleet with electric buses to reduce air pollution.
The new electric bus route connects the downtown area with the suburbs, promoting sustainable commuting.
Passengers on the electric bus enjoyed a quieter ride compared to traditional diesel-powered vehicles.
The government's initiative includes incentives for municipalities to adopt electric buses as part of their green transportation strategy.
Electric buses are gaining popularity worldwide due to their lower operational costs and environmental benefits.

senses.definition: a large public transportation vehicle designed to carry many passengers, typically a bus

examples.x: The omnibus stopped at multiple locations along the route.
She boarded the omnibus to commute to work downtown.
They installed new seats in the omnibus for added comfort.

senses.definition: a small bus designed to transport a small number of passengers, typically ranging from around 15 to 30 people

examples.x: The company rented a minicoach to shuttle employees to the conference venue.
We hired a minicoach for our family trip to comfortably accommodate everyone.
The school arranged for a minicoach to transport the students to the museum for their field trip.
The sports team traveled in a minicoach to their away game.
The tour group boarded a minicoach for a guided excursion around the city.

senses.definition: a small passenger-carrying vehicle that is larger than a typical car but smaller than a full-sized bus

examples.x: The airport shuttle service uses minibuses to transport passengers between the airport terminals and nearby hotels.
Many schools use minibuses to safely transport students to and from school or for field trips, accommodating a smaller group of students compared to a larger bus.
The school hired a minibus to transport students on their field trip to the science museum.
A minibus shuttles passengers between the airport and the nearby hotels, providing a convenient travel option.
The tour company offers guided city tours in a comfortable, air-conditioned minibus.

senses.definition: a small bus or van that operates on a flexible route and often picks up passengers at irregular intervals

examples.x: The jitney service provided transportation to remote areas not served by regular buses.
She hailed a jitney to get to the market on time.
They used a jitney to shuttle guests between the hotel and the wedding venue.
He appreciated the jitney's convenient schedule for his evening commute.
The jitney driver announced each stop loudly for passengers.

senses.definition: a public transportation vehicle that runs on tracks embedded in city streets

examples.x: The streetcar rattled along the tracks, stopping frequently to pick up passengers.
He used the streetcar to travel downtown for meetings.
The streetcar system expanded to cover more neighborhoods.
Tourists enjoy riding the historic streetcar through the city.
The streetcar provides convenient transportation across urban areas.

senses.definition: a bus that operates using electricity from overhead wires rather than an internal combustion engine

examples.x: The trolleybus glided silently along the city streets.
He rode the trolleybus to work every day to avoid traffic congestion.

senses.definition: a trolleybus that operates without tracks, using rubber tires on regular roadways

examples.x: The trackless trolley maneuvered easily through narrow streets.
She rode the trackless trolley to visit her friend across town.
He admired the efficiency of the trackless trolley system.
They upgraded the trackless trolley fleet with quieter engines.
The trackless trolley stopped at each intersection to pick up passengers.

senses.definition: a road-going vehicle consisting of a series of connected trailers or carriages, often used for transporting passengers in tourist areas

examples.x: The land train took tourists on a scenic tour around the historic city center.
During peak season, the land train operates daily to shuttle visitors between the museum and the beach.
The local park introduced a new land train service to make it easier for families to explore the extensive grounds.
We hopped on the land train for a leisurely ride through the botanical gardens.
The land train's route was specially designed to showcase the town's most famous landmarks.

senses.definition: a public transportation service that shuttles passengers between an airport and designated locations

examples.x: The airport bus arrived promptly at Terminal 3 to transport passengers downtown.
Passengers can save money by taking the airport bus instead of a taxi.
The airport bus service operates 24 hours a day for travelers' convenience.
She took the airport bus to the hotel after landing late at night.
The airport bus stop is located just outside the arrivals hall for easy access.

senses.definition: a type of bus that has only one floor

examples.x: The single-decker bus arrived right on time to take passengers to the station.
We decided to ride the single-decker because it was less crowded than the double-decker.
The children were excited to board the single-decker for their school trip.
The tour guide told us that the single-decker was more comfortable for the winding road.

senses.definition: a vehicle such as a bus, train, or ship with two levels on top of one another, providing additional seating capacity

examples.x: The double-decker bus offered tourists a panoramic view of the city from its upper deck.
In London, double-decker buses are an iconic part of the city's public transportation system.
The double-decker train was packed with commuters during rush hour, utilizing space efficiently.
The cruise ship featured a double-decker design with restaurants and lounges on the upper level.
Double-decker airplanes are used for long-haul flights, accommodating more passengers and offering additional amenities.

senses.definition: a temporary bus service replacing trains on a route due to maintenance or disruptions

examples.x: Due to track maintenance, a rail replacement bus service will operate between Station A and Station B this weekend.
Passengers should use the rail replacement bus service between 9 PM and 5 AM while upgrade works are carried out on the railway line.
The train service is suspended today; please take the rail replacement bus service from here to your destination.
Heavy snowfall has disrupted the train schedule; commuters are advised to use the rail replacement bus service until further notice.
Engineering works mean there will be a rail replacement bus service operating between major stations next Saturday and Sunday.

senses.definition: a small passenger vehicle pulled by a bicycle, commonly used in densely populated urban areas

examples.x: The cycle rickshaw weaved through traffic, transporting tourists to local attractions.
She negotiated the fare with the cycle rickshaw driver before starting the journey.
He hired a cycle rickshaw to explore the bustling market.
They promoted cycle rickshaws as a sustainable transportation option.
The cycle rickshaw driver pedaled steadily through the crowded streets.

senses.definition: a three-wheeled, motorized vehicle used for public transportation in many countries, especially in South and Southeast Asia

examples.x: The autorickshaw driver navigated through the crowded streets of Delhi with expert skill.
We took an autorickshaw to the market to avoid the heavy traffic.
The autorickshaw sputtered to a stop outside the train station, its engine overheated.
The autorickshaw's colorful decorations and bright lights made it stand out among the other vehicles on the road.

senses.definition: a series of connected carriages that travel on a railroad, often pulled by a locomotive

examples.x: I always enjoy listening to music while traveling on the train.
I missed my train, so I had to catch the next one.
He prefers traveling by train because it’s more relaxing than driving.
They waved goodbye from the platform as the train departed.
The train traveled through beautiful countryside.

senses.definition: a type of railway operation where trains run directly on urban streets shared with road traffic

examples.x: The city council debated the safety implications of having a street running train in downtown.
Pedestrians must be cautious when crossing streets with a street running train nearby.

senses.definition: an underground railroad system, typically in a big city

examples.x: I like reading a book during my subway ride.
I prefer taking the subway instead of driving in the city.
The subway map helped me navigate the different lines.
There are designated seats for elderly and pregnant passengers on the subway.

senses.definition: an underground railway system designed for public transportation within a city

examples.x: He used an app to check the metro schedule.
Using the metro is often the fastest way to travel across large urban centers.
The metro is an eco-friendly alternative to driving a car in the city.
The metro was crowded during the morning rush hour.

senses.definition: the transfer of people on a public transportation vehicle
public transportation systems designed to move large numbers of people efficiently, typically including buses, trains, subways, and light rail

examples.x: The city's mass transit system includes an extensive network of buses and subways.
Many commuters prefer using mass transit to avoid traffic congestion.
The government is investing in expanding the light rail as part of its mass transit initiative.
Mass transit plays a crucial role in reducing carbon emissions in urban areas.
High-speed trains are becoming increasingly popular as a sustainable form of mass transit.

senses.definition: a high-capacity public transportation system designed for fast, efficient travel within urban areas, such as subways or elevated trains

examples.x: The rapid transit train whisked commuters across the city in minutes.
She relied on rapid transit to avoid traffic jams during rush hour.
He used the rapid transit system to attend meetings downtown.
They expanded the rapid transit network with new subway lines.
The rapid transit station was crowded with passengers during peak times.

senses.definition: a form of urban passenger transportation with electrically powered trains on dedicated tracks, featuring smaller vehicles and shorter trains than heavy rail systems

examples.x: The new light rail transit line will connect suburbs to downtown.
The light rail transit system in San Diego is widely used by commuters.
Cities like Calgary and Denver have successful light rail transit networks.
Plans are underway to expand the light rail transit system in Minneapolis.

senses.definition: a facility where people can park their cars and then use public transportation

examples.x: Park and ride facilities are convenient for commuters who prefer to avoid city traffic.
The park and ride system helps reduce congestion in urban areas by encouraging carpooling.
Many cities around the world have implemented park and ride schemes to promote sustainable transportation options.
Using a park and ride service can save both time and money for daily commuters.
The effectiveness of a park and ride setup depends on its location and accessibility to public transit routes.

senses.definition: a fixed way between two places, along which a bus, plane, ship, etc. regularly travels

examples.x: She looked at the route map before her road trip.
The airline launched a new route to Tokyo last month.
The bus followed its usual route despite the heavy snow.
The cruise ship followed a route along the Mediterranean coast.

senses.definition: a special lane on a road only for buses, allowing them to move faster and more easily through traffic

examples.x: Cars are not allowed to drive in the bus lane.
The city added a new bus lane to reduce traffic.
He got a ticket for using the bus lane illegally.
The bus lane was empty during rush hour.
Cyclists sometimes use the bus lane.

senses.definition: a dedicated route or corridor reserved exclusively for public transportation vehicles

examples.x: The city introduced a new transitway to speed up bus routes and reduce traffic congestion.
Cyclists are not allowed on the transitway, which is reserved exclusively for public transport.
The transitway features dedicated lanes and priority signals to ensure faster travel for buses.
Commuters appreciate the reliability and efficiency of the new transitway system.
Transitways are crucial in cities aiming to improve public transit and reduce car dependency.

senses.definition: a train or bus that travels more quickly than usual because it only makes a few stops
a means of transportation that frequently travels between two places
a bus, train, etc. that makes all or most of the regular stops, allowing people to get on or off
of a public transportation route that connects two distant points without requiring a transfer

examples.x: They advertised the through service as the fastest way to reach the beach.
The through route was popular among commuters for its reliability.

senses.definition: (of a flight, train, journey etc.) having or making no stops

examples.x: The nonstop bus service between the two cities runs every two hours.
They completed a nonstop drive across the country without taking any long breaks.

senses.definition: a means of transportation that is used by a passenger after getting off a previous one to continue their journey
the act of leaving, usually to begin a journey

examples.x: The departure of the train was delayed by half an hour.
She bid farewell to her family before her departure for college.
The departure of the ship from the harbor marked the beginning of their cruise vacation.
They gathered at the airport to see off their friend before her departure for overseas.
He packed his bags in anticipation of his departure for the backpacking trip.

senses.definition: a place where a train or bus usually stops for passengers to get on or off
a place where multiple buses begin and end their journeys, particularly a journey between towns or cites

examples.x: She arrived at the bus station early to ensure she wouldn’t miss her bus to the city.
The bus station was crowded with travelers, all waiting for their buses to arrive.
He bought a ticket at the bus station for a trip that would take him across the country.
The city plans to upgrade the bus station, making it more comfortable and accessible for passengers.
After missing her bus, she decided to wait at the bus station for the next one to arrive.

senses.definition: a place at the side of a road that is usually marked with a sign, where buses regularly stop for passengers

examples.x: She waited at the bus stop for nearly twenty minutes before her bus finally arrived.
The new bus stop features a shelter to protect passengers from the rain and sun.
He always checks the bus schedule at the bus stop to ensure he doesn't miss his ride.
The bus stop near the school is often crowded with students after classes let out.
They decided to walk to the next bus stop, hoping it would be less busy than the one they were at.

senses.definition: a type of public transport where passengers must signal the driver if they want the vehicle to stop at a particular point along the route

examples.x: On some rural bus routes, passengers have to signal the driver for a request stop at smaller villages.
The train only stops at request stops when passengers indicate they need to get on or off.
Request stops help buses and trains stick to schedules in less populated areas.
Passengers should signal clearly at request stops to ensure the vehicle stops for them.
Tourists might find request stops confusing since they have to notify the driver to stop.

senses.definition: the last stop of a transportation line or route

examples.x: Passengers disembarked as the train reached its terminus.
From the city center, the subway line extends to the terminus located in the suburbs.
Located on the waterfront, the pier serves as the terminus for the ferry.

senses.definition: a building where trains, buses, planes, or ships start or finish their journey

examples.x: There were shops and restaurants inside the terminal for passengers to enjoy.
He met his friend at the terminal after a long journey.
A taxi stand is located just outside the terminal.

senses.definition: a place or building where we can get on or off a train or bus

examples.x: I arrived early at the station to make sure I didn't miss my train.

senses.definition: a small place where transport vehicles, such as buses or trains, stop to unload their passengers and content
a time of day at which traffic is the heaviest because people are leaving for work or home

examples.x: She left the house early to avoid the rush hour traffic on her way to the office.
The highway was congested during rush hour, causing a long delay for commuters.
The local news reported on the construction project affecting rush hour traffic patterns.
They decided to take public transportation to avoid the stress of driving during rush hour.
She planned her errands around rush hour to avoid getting stuck in traffic.

senses.definition: the time period during which the highest volume of traffic or activity occurs

examples.x: During peak hour, the city's main highways are often clogged with bumper-to-bumper traffic.
Many restaurants offer special deals outside of peak hour to attract customers during quieter times.
Public transportation systems are usually more crowded and run more frequently during peak hour.
It is advisable to avoid scheduling important meetings during peak hour due to the increased likelihood of delays.
Energy consumption tends to be higher during peak hour as more people are using appliances and electronics simultaneously.

senses.definition: a list or chart that shows the times at which trains, buses, planes, etc. leave and arrive

examples.x: She checked the train schedule to plan her commute to work.
They consulted the flight schedule to find the most convenient departure time for their trip.
The bus schedule indicated that the next bus would arrive in 15 minutes.
The ferry schedule outlined the departure times for trips between the islands.

senses.definition: a list or chart that shows the departure and arrival times of trains, buses, airplanes, etc.

examples.x: She checked the train timetable to plan her journey.
The bus timetable indicates that the next one arrives in 15 minutes.
He missed his train because he didn’t look at the timetable carefully.
You can find the subway timetable online for easy access.
The timetable lists all available bus routes in the city.

senses.definition: a physical location, usually at a transportation station or venue, where tickets for transportation services or events are sold or issued

examples.x: I bought my train tickets at the ticket office.
The ticket office opens an hour before the event starts.
He stood in line at the ticket office to purchase his concert tickets.
There was a long queue at the ticket office before the concert.
The ticket office was busy as everyone tried to get their boarding passes.

senses.definition: a piece of paper or card that shows you can do or get something, like ride on a bus or attend an event

examples.x: He lost his ticket and had to get a new one at the ticket counter.
I bought a ticket to the movie theater to watch the latest film.
The flight attendant scanned my electronic ticket before I boarded the plane.
The ticket for the bus is valid for a single journey.
They checked our tickets at the entrance of the stadium.

senses.definition: an online ticket that can be received electronically instead of a paper ticket

examples.x: She received her E-ticket via email for the concert she was attending.
The airline allows passengers to check in using their e-ticket on their mobile devices.
With an E-ticket, there is no need to print a physical copy for entry.
The e-ticket included a QR code for easy admission to the venue.
He scanned his E-ticket at the airport for quick boarding.

senses.definition: a ticket with which a passenger can continue their journey on another means of transportation
a ticket that allows entry to multiple events, games, or transport services during a set period, often at a discounted price

examples.x: She bought a season ticket for all the football matches this year.
He uses his season ticket to watch every performance at the theater.
They renewed their season ticket for access to the local swimming pool.
The park introduced a season ticket for frequent visitors.
He proudly showed his season ticket at the concert venue entrance.

senses.definition: a card or ticket allowing unlimited or specific rides on buses within a defined period

examples.x: In the city, many commuters use a bus pass to travel to work every day.
Sarah always keeps her bus pass in her wallet so she can use it whenever she needs to catch a bus.
Buying a monthly bus pass is often cheaper than paying for individual bus tickets every day.
Students can get a discounted bus pass from their school, which helps them save money on transportation.
Don't forget to renew your bus pass before the end of the month to avoid any inconvenience.

senses.definition: permitting travel to a place without return

examples.x: His job offer included a one-way flight.
He planned a one-way cruise to the Caribbean.
They embarked on a one-way road trip across the country.

senses.definition: a delay or obstruction that prevents progress or causes a situation to be temporarily halted

examples.x: There was a hold-up on the highway due to a multi-car accident, causing traffic to back up for miles.
The construction project faced a hold-up when they discovered an unexpected underground utility line.
The meeting experienced a hold-up because the keynote speaker's flight was delayed.
The approval of the new policy has encountered a hold-up due to disagreements among the board members.
The hold-up in the supply chain has led to a shortage of key components for the manufacturing process.

senses.definition: the period a vehicle, such as a bus or train, remains stationary at a stop to allow passengers to board and alight

examples.x: Transit agencies aim to reduce dwell time to improve overall service efficiency.
Excessive dwell time at busy stations can cause delays and disrupt schedules.
Passengers' boarding and alighting speed significantly influences the dwell time at each stop.
Design features like wide doors and low floors can help minimize dwell time for public transport vehicles.
Accurate data on dwell time can aid in optimizing transit operations and scheduling.

senses.definition: designed or scheduled to occur during peak commuting hours when traffic is heaviest

examples.x: The drive-time radio show was tailored to entertain listeners stuck in traffic with lively discussions and upbeat music.
The drive-time slot for the TV news broadcast ensured maximum viewership among commuters returning home in the evening.
His drive-time commute from the suburbs to downtown often tested his patience due to heavy traffic congestion on the highway.
The drive-time delivery service guarantees packages arrive before 10 AM, ensuring businesses receive their shipments promptly each morning.
The drive-time menu at the café featured quick and portable breakfast options ideal for commuters grabbing a bite on their way to work.

senses.definition: the quality and quantity of service provided to users, typically measured in terms of performance indicators such as speed, reliability, and accessibility

examples.x: The level of service at the new restaurant exceeded our expectations with prompt seating, attentive waitstaff, and delicious food served within minutes.
The company prides itself on maintaining a high level of service, ensuring that customer inquiries are addressed promptly and efficiently.
As a commuter, I rely heavily on the subway's level of service; delays and overcrowding can significantly impact my daily schedule.
The airline's level of service suffered during the storm, resulting in delayed flights and frustrated passengers.
The car rental agency's level of service declined after it switched to a new reservation system, causing long wait times at pick-up.
